---
title: "Planning"
---

What we just did in the previous section, letting the agent have full control of a multi-step process, is not the best way to fully leverage Agentic coding capabilities. Instead, it is recommended to break down your workflow into smaller, distinct increments that are easier to verify and iterate on.


## Plan mode overview

This is precisely where "Plan mode" becomes invaluable. Plan mode allows you to refine your instructions for your agent, resolve potential ambiguities, and quickly redirect overeager agents. This gives you better control over the different components of your analytical workflow before any code or files are modified in your project. 

To effectively integrate Agentic AI into your work, consider following this structured approach:

- Begin in Plan mode (read-only)
- Review the proposed steps for your analysis 
- Supply additional context and refine the plan
- Iterate through this cycle as many times as needed
- Ask for separate scripts for the primary components of your workflow
- Once satisfied, switch to Build mode for actual implementation
- leverage version control to create snapshots of your work before and after major changes


## Switching to plan mode

In opencode, to switch from the "build" mode (default when you start opencode) to the "plan" mode you can use the <kbd>tab</kbd> key.

::: {.callout-note}
## Model Choice

For complex, multi-step problem planning, it is advisable to leverage the most advanced model available, keeping token costs in mind. After the initial steps are laid out, transitioning to a less advanced model to execute those predefined tasks can lead to a more efficient use of your resources.
:::


## Planning our NOAA tides example

Let's restart our NOAA tides example, this time leveraging Plan mode.

1. Start a new session `/new`
1. Switch to Plan mode <kbd>tab</kbd>
1. Select your model `/models` and choose the `Gemini 3.1 Pro` model
1. Enter the following prompt:

> I would like to download the monthly mean high water for all NOAA stations in California using the NOAA API and python.

![Plan mode example - prompt answer](../images/03-plan_mode-prompt1.png){fig-alt="Plan mode prompt answer showing the various proposed steps and supplemtal infomration requests"}

> Download data for the last decade, use the MLLW datum as reference, use the metric system for units, create a data folder with a subfolder data-raw in it and save the downloaded data in this subfolder. Write a Python script to download the data and save it in a code folder at the top of our project folder

![Plan mode example - updated answer](../images/03-plan_mode-prompt2.png){fig-alt="Plan mode prompt answer update to incorporate the new information"}

_and repeat until you are satisfy!_

:::{.callout-tip}
## Challenge

Your turn!

_As before, please complete this exercise individually but discuss results in small groups (2-5 people)._

#### Our ask

1. Before you start prompting, think about the various steps you need
1. Refine the best your can the latest prompt you used previously
1. make sure you selected the `Gemini 3.1 Pro` model
1. Switch to Plan mode
1. Enter your prompts
1. Provide the requested information
1. Iterate until you are satisfied
1. Switch to Build mode and run you workflow!
1. Look at the results (code, data, outputs) and iterate/adjust as needed
1. Don't forget to commit relevant files to your git history before rerunning your workflow

**Once you are satisfy, switch to build and implement the plan**
:::





## Project documentation

Before we end our work on this project for the day, we want to make sure to document what we have done so far.

1. Check that the current folder and files structure of your project mataches what you had in mind
1. Make sure that all the scripts that have been created have been committed to your git history
1. Write a concise `README.md` with information about the project: goals, file structure, and how to run the code are good elements to include. Add and commmit this file to your git history.


**All the information you are adding in your project directory will be included in the context that will be send to the model with your prompts, so it is beneficial to add as much information as you have while working on your project.**

::: {.callout-note}
Opencode can assist you with all these various steps!
:::


## Sessions management
